steps.title: Extract the equation from the graphed line
steps.content:
Identify two points on the line: $(0, -2)$ and $(4, -10)$.

Calculate the slope:
$$m = \frac{-10 - (-2)}{4 - 0} = \frac{-8}{4} = -2$$

The $y$-intercept is $-2$, so the equation of the graphed line is:
$$y = -2x - 2$$

steps.title: Match the form to the choices
steps.content: Rewrite the result as $f(x) = 17 - 2x$. This is in the form $f(x) = d - cx$ where $d = 17$ and $c = 2$, both positive constants. That matches choice A — A is correct.
